How to Use the Torus Instructor Dashboard
RISE Rwanda

RISE Rwanda · OLI Torus

How to Use the Torus
Instructor Dashboard

A teaching-cycle guide. Use the dashboard before, during, and after meeting with learners to turn data into action.

Before Meeting with Learners
Prepare for your next session.
  • How are students at my learning site doing?
  • Which students may need support?
  • How do I reach out to students who need support?
  • What concepts are students struggling with?
During Meeting with Learners
Use data to guide instruction.
  • What misconceptions should I address today?
  • Where should I spend most of the meeting time?
After Meeting with Learners
Reflect and improve.
  • Did students understand the material?
  • What should I improve next time?

Before Meeting with Learners

How are students at my learning site doing?

From the Insights tab, open the Learning Dashboard and set the view to Entire Course. The Course Summary shows average proficiency, assessment score, and student progress, alongside an AI recommendation on where to focus next.

Steps

  • 1Open the Dashboard.
  • 2Review the Course Summary.
  • 3Review the AI recommendation.
  • 4Check overall progress and completion rates.

Before Meeting with Learners

Which students may need support?

Scroll to the Student Support panel and apply the progress and proficiency filters. The donut chart groups learners as Struggling, On Track, Excelling, or N/A, and the list shows who needs attention, with a View Profile link for each one.

Steps

  • 1Apply filters as needed.
  • 2Review students who are behind or struggling.
  • 3Open student details for additional context.
  • 4Reach out when intervention is needed.

Before Meeting with Learners

How do I reach out to students who need support?

On the Student Support panel, switch between Active and Inactive learners and tick the ones you want to reach. Click Email Selected to message them, or open a name to review a student first.

Steps

  • 1Filter to the group you want to contact.
  • 2Select individual students, or choose Select all.
  • 3Click Email Selected to send your message.

Before Meeting with Learners

What concepts are students struggling with?

Open the Content section and review the Challenging Objectives card. It lists the learning objectives where students show low proficiency (40% or below) and expands into the sub-objectives behind each one.

Steps

  • 1Review Learning Objectives.
  • 2Open objectives with lower performance.
  • 3Open objectives with high performance.
  • 4Investigate related assessments and activities.

Note: data displays once students enroll.

During Meeting with Learners

What misconceptions should I address today?

Open a recent assessment or practice activity in Insights. Each question shows its percent correct, and expanding a row reveals the answer distribution, so you can see which incorrect options students chose.

Steps

  • 1Open recent assessments or practice activities.
  • 2Review answer distributions.
  • 3Discuss common incorrect answers and reasoning.

During Meeting with Learners

Where should I spend most of the meeting time?

Use the same Challenging Objectives card to compare objectives by proficiency. The lowest-scoring objectives show where to concentrate discussion and examples during the meeting.

Steps

  • 1Review challenging objectives.
  • 2Focus discussion and examples on those areas.

Note: data displays once students enroll.

After Meeting with Learners

Did students understand the material?

Open the Assessments card for a completed assessment. The score histogram and summary statistics (mean, median, and spread) show whether students understood the material and how consistent their results were.

Steps

  • 1Review assessment performance.
  • 2Examine objective-level results.
  • 3Identify patterns across the course.

After Meeting with Learners

What should I improve next time?

Go to Manage → Assessment Scores for a breakdown by student and assessment. Use Download CSV to export the data, then adjust instruction or pacing around the objectives that consistently underperform.

Steps

  • 1Review objectives that consistently show lower performance.
  • 2Export data if deeper analysis is needed.
  • 3Adjust instruction, activities, or pacing. Use the AI recommendation as guidance for action.
